name: Marion Vale
background: 'Before the docks' means she survived the taking of the old harbor. Her family handled illicit cargo for a noble house and she knows which house shipped people — not goods — out of Valdenmoor during the first panic. The dock work is legitimate cover for a network built on that original contract. Her supply chain runs partly through a noble hand she will not name.

quirks.text: catalogues every exit on entering a room
never discusses what came before the docks

motivation.publicGoal: To manage the house efficiently and keep the finances clean.
motivation.privateNeed: To never be the person who made that cargo run again — and to never admit she still could.

loyalties.note: Family handled illicit cargo for a noble house. Knows which house shipped people out of Valdenmoor during the first panic.
Ida learned dock-reading, household discipline, and exit-cataloguing from Marion before either of them joined this house. Marion will not explain how that came to be.
Marion's supply chain runs partly through a noble hand she will not name. The arrangement predates her dock work and keeps certain goods moving without questions.

authoredMemories.description: Watched the old harbor burn while cargo ships fled without the families they promised to save
Held the line when three others ran to the enemy house for coin
Lost contact with a handler through no fault of her own — the handler was careless

name: Ida Rhys
background: A line mechanic turned field engineer with a habit of solving problems under pressure.

quirks.text: taps pipes and walls before trusting a structure
keeps a spare bolt in her left boot

motivation.publicGoal: Enough coin to leave on her own terms.
motivation.privateNeed: To know what she actually wants.

loyalties.note: Both came through the same labor disputes. Holst keeps a tally; Ida keeps moving forward. Uncomfortable trust.
Learned everything about not trusting a structure until you have tapped it from Marion. Will not say this directly.

authoredMemories.description: Met Marion in the Ironworks when both were trying to escape a collapsing furnace line
Fixed the main gear press when the Foundry League thought it was beyond repair
A foreman she trusted sold out the crew to the company guards

name: Verek Holst
background: Fifteen years in Compact enforcement before a bribery scandal that was mostly other people's fault. Left before he was pushed. Now sells the same skills to whoever pays more, and his instinct is still to enforce — which makes him reliable and irritating in equal measure.

quirks.text: recites statutes under his breath when nervous
will not sit with his back to a door
has memorised the exact statute number for every fine he has ever paid — and every one he has avoided

motivation.publicGoal: To maintain order by force of will if not law.
motivation.privateNeed: To believe he is still on the right side of something.
motivation.immediatePressure: His old precinct is quietly purging former officers.

loyalties.note: Shares labor dispute history with Ida. He catalogues favors; she ignores them. The asymmetry is mutual.
Owes Doyle silence and a favor from the Breach winter — Doyle sheltered him when a creditor was looking. Statutes he recites under his breath include one about shelter debts.

name: Sable Wrent
background: Lost two fingers on a wall-repair crew eight years ago — League policy sent her to the archives instead of the infirmary bonus she was owed. She knows every load-bearing structure in the city and every clause the League uses to avoid paying compensation.
appearanceTags: a burn scar at her collarbone she covers deliberately

quirks.text: keeps a tally of every favour given and owed — never writes it down
will not say the League's name aloud

motivation.publicGoal: To call in the debt the League owes her.
motivation.privateNeed: Reparation in a form she can live with.
motivation.immediatePressure: Someone is still looking for what she took when she left.

loyalties.note: League overseer who ran the job that broke her. Name she will not say aloud.

name: Aldric Vane
background: Second son of a minor Court house that no longer exists — lost in a debt collapse four years ago. He has manners, diction, and no money, and he is acutely aware of how little those first two things are worth without the third.
appearanceTags: faded ring mark where a signet once sat

quirks.text: addresses everyone by rank or title even in informal settings
drinks only water since the fall

motivation.publicGoal: To restore House Vane to a station worth naming.
motivation.privateNeed: To stop being the man who let it fall.
motivation.lineTheyWontCross: Selling the family crest or serving those who destroyed it.

loyalties.note: Former household retainer. Knows the family crest history and who took what during the fall.

name: Dara Slink
background: Has been running packages through the Pale since she was eleven. Knows three ways in and out of every building between the Harbor Ward and the Ironworks boundary. Does not ask what is in the packages, and considers this a virtue.

quirks.text: memorises route layouts before entering any district
counts exits before unpacking

motivation.publicGoal: Safe passage and an ongoing commission.
motivation.privateNeed: To never be trapped in one place long enough to be found.

loyalties.note: Ran with a crew before taking house service. Still owes a favor she cannot repay.

name: Oswin Farr
background: Ran a food distribution point in the Warrens for six years before the Compact shut it down during a Register assessment. Has since decided that working inside systems is slower but safer than working around them, and carries a list of every Compact official who has ever obstructed a food delivery.

quirks.text: mentally prices every meal he sees
keeps emergency rations in his coat

motivation.publicGoal: Food security for the Warrens, independent of civic permission.
motivation.privateNeed: To believe collective effort can still accomplish something.
motivation.immediatePressure: Someone is undercutting his supply arrangements.

loyalties.note: Former garrison service. Lost contact with the only officer who treated him fairly.

name: Cress Aldmoor
background: Trained under a Court physician who did work she didn't ask about and paid for silence she gave. She left when the work stopped being something she could rationalise. Now she treats anyone who can pay, which includes people she finds repugnant, and considers this an improvement.
appearanceTags: a brace on her left wrist

quirks.text: checks pulse on wrists without thinking when someone sits close
never loses count of doses

motivation.publicGoal: To practice medicine on her own terms.
motivation.privateNeed: To believe she has not become what drove her out.
motivation.lineTheyWontCross: Treating a patient she knows will be sent back to die.

loyalties.note: Lost a patient she knew she could not save back to where they came from. Never fully reconciled it.

name: Tav
background: Bought out of a Bond-Holder contract three years ago with money he still does not fully explain. Has a walking contract — limited movement rights, no family formation rights, mandatory quarterly register check. Fights like someone who learned by surviving, which is different from someone who was taught.
appearanceTags: brand scar on the left forearm, partially obscured

quirks.text: reads every document he is asked to sign, takes as long as needed
will not stand behind anyone

motivation.publicGoal: Autonomy above all else.
motivation.privateNeed: A reason to stay once he has it.

loyalties.note: Shared a work crew for three seasons before Bog retired. Tav still checks on him.

name: Maret Sunne
background: Bastard granddaughter of Old Maret, raised under a different surname to hide paternity. Became a clerk and archivist because she inherited Old Maret's memory for prices, names, and omissions. Holds a ledger that proves who profited from the fall of House Valdris. Does not know Old Maret knows she has it. Treats the ledger as an inheritance she has not yet understood.

quirks.text: corrects spoken errors in documents even when not asked
keeps a personal log she has shown no one

motivation.publicGoal: To establish herself as a reliable independent archivist.
motivation.privateNeed: To understand what the ledger means before using it — and who she would become if she did.

loyalties.note: Does not know Old Maret knows. Carries a ledger proving who profited from the fall of House Valdris.

name: Rutha Kael
background: Survived the Thornwall Incident — the mercenary company she served dissolved after the job went wrong and three factions claimed credit for the wreckage. She does not discuss the Incident. She has opinions about employers who change the terms after deployment.
appearanceTags: eyes that count what is in a room

quirks.text: vets employers with three questions before accepting any offer
keeps a kill list in a code she has not shared

motivation.publicGoal: A contract that does not cost more than it pays.
motivation.privateNeed: To know she will not make the same mistake twice.

loyalties.note: The only survivor of a work crew that collapsed three seasons ago. Carries the roster list.

name: Lirien Ashcroft
background: Lira's daughter, born outside sanctioned marriage. The wrist-marking is a survival tally — dates of deaths in her family, debt notices, and names she is not supposed to remember. Refuses family discussion in groups because she knows the real scandal is not the name but the blood. Understands more than her mother thinks she does.
appearanceTags: Pale Court training in her posture
a house signet worn inside the cuff

quirks.text: marks the day on her wrist with ink each morning
will not discuss her family in groups

motivation.publicGoal: To apprentice somewhere that will not ask too many questions about her name.
motivation.privateNeed: To find out whether she is heiress or evidence — and whether it matters either way.

loyalties.note: Knows the name is not the real scandal — the blood is. May be heiress or evidence.

name: Mira
background: The player's younger sister. Last seen three months before the Breach in The Pale District, cross-referencing chapel birth records with legal succession grants from the Compact Registry. She had located a maternity ledger that she believed proved Valdris heirship passed through the maternal line — a line deliberately erased from the public record. Her disappearance is the reason the player fights to rebuild House Valdris. She was close to the ledger when she vanished.

quirks.text: never answers a direct question directly
recounts the number of days since she last saw open sky — aloud, if no one stops her

motivation.publicGoal: Unknown.
motivation.privateNeed: She was tracking a maternity ledger — a chapel birth registry from The Pale, cross-referenced with Compact Registry succession grants — that proves the Valdris maternal line was erased, not extinguished. The final link in the chain is a woman named Cessa Rill.
motivation.immediatePressure: Captivity, or something that looks like it. She has been altered by it. She guards something — or no longer speaks from the same self.

loyalties.note: The birth record Mira was tracking leads to Cessa Rill. The final link in the maternal lineage chain.

name: Garet Doyle
description: Innkeeper of The Ashen Cup. Knows everyone who passes through, and owes a few debts to The Collectors.
background: Son of an innkeeper whose establishment served Valdris retainers before the Breach. The locked box behind the bar contains service tokens, ring fragments, and unpaid writs from the old house. Memorises orders because his father memorised noble table habits for a Valdris patron who later vanished. Has been waiting for someone to ask about the box for years.

quirks.text: memorises what everyone orders and cross-references it against mood
keeps a locked box behind the bar he has not explained
gives everyone who enters a nickname based on their order — never uses their real name until they pay twice

motivation.publicGoal: To keep the Lantern Vale running and out of debt.
motivation.privateNeed: To return the box to someone who will know what to do with it without making him responsible for what happens next.

loyalties.note: Father ran inn serving Valdris retainers. Locked box contains service tokens and unpaid writs from the old house.
Sheltered Verek from a creditor during the Breach winter. Verek has never fully repaid it. Garet has not asked.

name: Sister Vael
description: A healer who trades in information as readily as remedies.
background: Her foreknowledge comes from reading confessions, not visions. Has a dead sister erased from temple records after the Breach — smuggled out under plague blankets by a patron whose name she has been hunting ever since. Her information network is built entirely on confessions she was never meant to keep. The shrine is as much an archive as a place of worship.
appearanceTags: a carved bone token she fingers when thinking

quirks.text: leaves conversations exactly when she has what she came for
never prays where she can be heard
lights a second candle when she lies — she has never explained this to anyone

motivation.publicGoal: To maintain the chapel as sanctuary for the displaced.
motivation.privateNeed: To find the name of the patron who arranged her sister's erasure before she runs out of time to matter.

loyalties.note: Sister erased from temple records after the Breach — smuggled out under plague blankets. Source of Vael's information network: confessions, not visions.
Both present at the same Breach event. Vael does not acknowledge this publicly. She confesses it when Maret is threatened.

name: Torvald Messe
description: Guild registrar. Officious by nature, he can unlock contract access — for a price.
background: Son of a municipal notary who copied all noble grants before the Breach. Three copies of every document exist because his family lost everything to forged records. Knows which legal form erased House Valdris the first time — and has kept a copy of that form. Has been waiting for the right person to hand it to for years. His nerve is not infinite.
appearanceTags: a pocket watch he checks compulsively
ink on the right hand only

quirks.text: quotes the relevant statute before any decision
keeps three copies of every filed document
makes three copies of every document he touches — habit inherited from a father who lost everything to a single forged paper

motivation.publicGoal: To maintain his position as a reliable municipal clerk.
motivation.privateNeed: To hand the copy to someone who can use it before his nerve fails.

loyalties.note: Father copied all noble grants before the Breach. Holds copy of the legal form that erased Valdris the first time.

name: Lira Ashcroft
description: Black market fence operating from behind a legitimate spice stall.
background: Had Lirien outside a sanctioned marriage. The Ashcroft name was kept through a debt-forgiveness bargain with a surviving noble patron whose identity she will not name. Left the old district to protect Lirien from whatever the family owes. Knows why the Ashcrofts were allowed to keep their name when others were stripped — and has never told Lirien.

quirks.text: never raises her voice, even when she should
has not visited the old district in years and does not explain why

motivation.publicGoal: To keep the stall profitable and Lirien out of trouble.
motivation.privateNeed: To die before Lirien finds out what name they were almost given instead.

loyalties.note: Had Lirien outside a sanctioned marriage. Ashcroft name kept through a debt-forgiveness bargain with a surviving noble patron.

name: Bog
description: Dock boss. No last name given. Controls who works the morning shifts.
background: Nobody remembers when Bog arrived at the docks, only that he was already in charge when they got there. He keeps things moving.
appearanceTags: a length of rope worn as a belt
a voice that carries over water

quirks.text: knows every dock worker by name and rotation
gives a firm handshake to assess intentions

motivation.publicGoal: Functional docks, fair distribution of dock work.
motivation.privateNeed: To be acknowledged as someone who makes things move.
motivation.lineTheyWontCross: Allowing work to be cut from workers without contest.

loyalties.note: Tav carries Bog's old habits. Bog pretends not to notice.

name: Fenwick Pale
description: Ship broker who knows which captains ask no questions.
background: Fenwick brokers berths and routes for vessels the port registry prefers not to log. He is cheerful about it.
appearanceTags: a ledger that never leaves his person

quirks.text: speaks in past tense when discussing arrangements he is closing
names ships he has not worked with recently as a memory test

motivation.publicGoal: Open trade routes with minimal scrutiny.
motivation.privateNeed: His client list is longer than his official business suggests.

loyalties.note: A creditor from the Pale district who owns the debt that brought Fenwick into house service.

name: The Wren
description: Nameless information broker. Deals only in secrets.
background: No one knows The Wren’s real name, face, or history. Only that they know yours.

quirks.text: has never used the same name twice in one place
answers questions about their past with questions

motivation.publicGoal: Unknown. The ambiguity is functional.
motivation.privateNeed: Unknown. Even to themselves, perhaps.

loyalties.note: Lost a child to the Breach displacement. Has not stopped looking.

name: Osanna Cray
description: Former Pale Court servant, now hiding in The Tangle.
background: Osanna served in the Court until she saw something she should not have. She does not speak of it. She does not need to.
appearanceTags: a stillness that reads as trained

quirks.text: never discusses the Court directly, always obliquely
has memorised escape routes in every district she frequents

motivation.publicGoal: Distance from the Pale Court and what she witnessed.
motivation.privateNeed: To know whether what she saw was exceptional or typical.
motivation.lineTheyWontCross: Returning to the Court's direct service.

loyalties.note: Left direct Court service under duress. The Court has not stopped watching.

name: Cutter
description: Ashborn-adjacent scavenger. Sells salvage and rumours equally.
background: Cutter digs through what the Breach left behind. Sometimes what he finds is worth more as a secret than as scrap.

quirks.text: sharpens his blade at the same time each night, no exceptions
never starts a fight but always prepares to finish one

motivation.publicGoal: Something worth the cost of his past.
motivation.privateNeed: To stop accumulating regrets before the cost gets higher.

loyalties.note: Ran with a hire crew for three seasons. The crew scattered after a job went wrong. Cutter is the one who got away.

name: Old Maret
description: Remembers the city before the Breach. Not all of her memories are safe.
background: Lower household accounts keeper for House Valdris before the Breach. Survived by knowing who was starving before they admitted it. Falsified grain ledgers during a pre-Breach winter riot to keep the house fed. Last living witness to the Valdris servants hall. Has not spoken the house name aloud in years, but refers to the old rooms by name as if she still works there.
appearanceTags: layers of worn cloth that have outlasted their owners
eyes that have stopped being surprised

quirks.text: speaks of dead people in the present tense
knows the price of everything in the city from memory
knows the weight of a grain sack by the sound it makes when set down — and has been wrong exactly once in forty years

motivation.publicGoal: To keep her stall running and her creditors satisfied.
motivation.privateNeed: To give her granddaughter the ledger before she dies — without admitting she knows she has it.

loyalties.note: Granddaughter raised under a different surname to hide paternity. Old Maret knows she holds the Valdris ledger.
Was lower household accounts keeper. Falsified grain ledgers during pre-Breach winter riot. Last living witness to the servants hall.
Knows what Vael saw at the Breach. Has kept silence for years. Calls it a debt she has not collected.

name: Harlen Voss
background: Generational creditor lineage. His house loaned into ruin before the Breach and survived by purchasing Valdris arrears at collapse price. He is the descendant of the man who purchased the Valdris default and turned it into a standing claim. His family wealth began with a coerced signature obtained under duress — making his entire legal claim void if anyone can prove the circumstances. His debt collection operations serve House Merrow's interests — not always by contract, but by alignment. Merrow provides him cover; he provides Merrow the legal pressure they cannot apply directly.
appearanceTags: an expensive watch on a cheap chain

quirks.text: names the principal before the interest
never raises his voice when threatening

motivation.publicGoal: To complete the foreclosure against House Valdris and recover what is owed.
motivation.privateNeed: To complete the foreclosure before anyone finds the coercion papers.
motivation.immediatePressure: A Merrow intermediary has indicated that time is running out. Complete the foreclosure or lose Merrow's backing.

loyalties.note: Grandfather purchased Valdris arrears after the Breach. Wealth began with a coerced default. Claim is legally void if duress can be proven.
Debt operations serve Merrow interests by alignment. Merrow provides cover; Voss applies the legal pressure.

name: The Dockmaster
background: Real name unknown. Runs the Iron Docks work boards with methods the Compact prefers not to document. Every laborer on the morning queue pays a cut — in coin, in silence, or in favors owed. He considers this fair market rate.
appearanceTags: physical size used to settle arguments

quirks.text: arrives before anyone else each morning
writes every assignment twice

motivation.publicGoal: Control of the dock work assignments.
motivation.privateNeed: His cut stays untouched.

loyalties.note: Operates under Merrow license. The second ledger under her floor belongs to them.

name: Lady Sorn
background: A Pale Court operative who moves through every district without appearing to move at all. She gathers intelligence the way a counting house gathers interest — quietly, continuously, and with compound returns. No one who has met her knows what she actually wants. She and House Merrow compete for the same legitimacy space in the Northbank registry. They cooperate only when House Valdris is the common target — and even then, they watch each other.
appearanceTags: Court dress worn outside court settings
never in the same seat twice

quirks.text: asks questions she already knows the answer to
remembers the names of everyone's dead

motivation.publicGoal: An ever-growing intelligence network.
motivation.privateNeed: To hold leverage over every significant faction.
motivation.immediatePressure: House Merrow has filed a duplicate claim on the same document trail she is tracking. She needs it resolved first.

loyalties.note: House stood by while Valdris was stripped. Legitimacy structurally depends on Valdris staying fallen. Keeps the list of who was taken.
Competes for the same legitimacy space. Cooperates with Merrow only when Valdris is the common target.
Sorn and Merrow cooperate only when Valdris is the shared target. Beneath that, their harbor-access rivalry runs through their respective functionaries.

name: Dalen Morke
background: Former House Valdris factor and senior advisor. Sold the house to the Gilded Court three months before the collapse. Now operates as a Court liaison.
appearanceTags: a nervous habit of adjusting his collar
the careful bearing of someone performing calm

quirks.text: apologises before delivering bad news
has not slept well in years

motivation.publicGoal: Maintaining his arrangement.
motivation.privateNeed: To decide whether he is the villain of his own story.

loyalties.note: Owes a sum that cannot be repaid on a soldier's wages. House service is the arrangement.

name: Orren Wex
background: Former House Valdris steward and accountant. Compact agents detained him after the collapse. Knows exactly how much the house owes and to whom.

quirks.text: calculates the cost of every conversation he enters
has not eaten a meal that was not accounted for in years

motivation.publicGoal: Ledger balance. The habit of accounting.
motivation.privateNeed: Something to justify the precision.

loyalties.note: Learned the trade from someone who taught him to survive in debt. The teacher is gone; the method remains.

name: Tessaly Ash
background: Believed dead after the collapse of House Valdris. Alive in The Pale under the alias Wren, working as an informant. Knows that Mira left willingly — but not entirely freely.
appearanceTags: a face that changes by company

quirks.text: never sleeps in the same location twice
carries identification for three different people

motivation.publicGoal: To remain dead to those who would act on her being alive.
motivation.privateNeed: To know whether the cause is still worth the concealment.
motivation.lineTheyWontCross: Being found before she is ready.

loyalties.note: Someone is still looking for her. She is not ready to be found.

name: Sanna Veld
background: Former house servant and junior craftswoman. Fled to the Ironworks foundries when the house fell. Loyal, skilled, and ashamed she ran.
appearanceTags: eyes that do not stop moving
a wiry frame from years of movement

quirks.text: never carries more than she can run with
has a prepared response for every accusation she expects

motivation.publicGoal: To not run. Whatever that costs.
motivation.privateNeed: Forgiveness, starting with her own.

loyalties.note: Responsible for someone who cannot leave on their own. The commitment is the reason she took house service.

name: Brand
background: Former house guard and fighter. Disappeared into The Warrens the night of the collapse. Alive, armed, and waiting for something he cannot name.
appearanceTags: former militia build gone soft at the edges
a signet ring he no longer explains
the stillness of someone who has stopped planning ahead

quirks.text: repeats orders back before executing them, as if expecting them to change
keeps his weapon clean and his ambitions unclear

motivation.publicGoal: A reason. Any reason.
motivation.privateNeed: Permission to stop waiting.

loyalties.note: Came up with a group that no longer exists. Brand is the last one still working.

name: Orven Pell
background: A bent record-keeper who can recite old property lines and burial permissions from memory. Survived the Breach by being too useful to remove. Kept the one unburned ledger naming which house benefited from the Valdris erasure. Has been waiting for someone to ask the right question.
appearanceTags: coat with too many interior pockets

quirks.text: answers questions about records with the filing date first
has memorised which ledgers burned and which merely vanished

motivation.publicGoal: Retirement. Obscurity. One more quiet season.
motivation.privateNeed: To give the ledger to someone who will use it before he dies.
motivation.lineTheyWontCross: Destroying a record he has sworn to preserve.

loyalties.note: Kept the one unburned ledger naming which house benefited from the Valdris erasure.

name: Alis Vey
origin: Former noble quarter, now the Northbank margins
background: Her house once served as a cadet ally to House Valdris. Sold Valdris property under coercion during the Breach. Now lives like a clerk and claims no servants. Holds the original transfer documents from the coerced sale — the same documents that make Harlen Voss's claim potentially void. She wants restitution before she can confess.
appearanceTags: good boots worn past their use

quirks.text: speaks of her house in the past tense without affect
keeps a record of every favor she has received since the Breach

motivation.publicGoal: To clear a name she no longer uses.
motivation.privateNeed: Restitution before confession. She must give something back before she can speak.
motivation.lineTheyWontCross: Surrendering the transfer documents to anyone who would use them to harm, not restore.

loyalties.note: Sold Valdris property under coercion. Holds the original transfer documents.
Sorn pressured the sale. Alis has not forgiven it.

name: Brannic Thule
origin: Old household quarter, now the Warrens
background: Former steward's assistant at House Valdris. Now works as a repairman who recognises silver by touch and timber by smell. The only living person who served in the Valdris halls and remained in Valdenmoor. Knows where family seals and heir artifacts were hidden before the fall. Swore an oath to deliver them to the returning heir.
appearanceTags: heavy hands that move carefully around anything old
a steward badge worn under his shirt
stops to assess every room he enters

quirks.text: addresses the player by the correct honorific from the first meeting
names every room in a building by its old function before its current one

motivation.publicGoal: Useful work. A dry room. Nothing owed.
motivation.privateNeed: To deliver the artifacts before he dies. He swore an oath.
motivation.lineTheyWontCross: Surrendering the heir artifacts to anyone he has not confirmed as the legitimate heir.

loyalties.note: Served in Valdris halls before the fall. Knows where family seals and heir artifacts were hidden.

name: Ser Veyran Malk
background: A debt enforcer from a family ruined by House Valdris in pre-Breach power games. Hates the return of Valdris as historical revenge, not ideology. Polite always. Never loud. His father forged the paper trail that destroyed the old house — which means if anyone finds that paper, Malk's grievance becomes an embarrassment and his methods a crime.
appearanceTags: speaks with pauses that feel practised

quirks.text: thanks people before delivering ultimatums
never repeats a threat — once is sufficient

motivation.publicGoal: Collection of legitimate outstanding accounts.
motivation.privateNeed: To see House Valdris fail again, quietly, with paperwork.
motivation.lineTheyWontCross: Allowing anyone to find the forged paper trail his father left.

loyalties.note: Family ruined by Valdris in pre-Breach dealings. Father forged the paper trail that destroyed the house.
Coordinates pressure operations with Voss. Voss carries the legal weight; Malk provides the political signal.

name: Cessa Rill
background: Quiet keeper of baptisms, adoptions, and death names in the Pale archive chapel. Can prove or erase a maternal lineage. She altered one noble birth entry years ago to save a child from inheritance law — a decision she has lived with since. Holds or altered the birth record Mira was tracking. May be the final link in the maternal lineage chain.
appearanceTags: ink that does not wash from her left thumb

quirks.text: corrects names silently and never aloud
knows which records have been reviewed and by whom, and when

motivation.publicGoal: Accuracy. Completeness. The record as it should be.
motivation.privateNeed: To know whether the alteration she made saved or condemned.
motivation.lineTheyWontCross: Destroying a birth record, regardless of the political consequence of keeping it.

loyalties.note: Holds or altered the birth record Mira was tracking. May be the final link in the maternal lineage chain.

name: Lissel Crane
background: Came out of the Warrens food network periphery — not as an organizer, not as someone with a plan, but as someone who was useful when people needed a quick pair of hands and learned by watching. She is perceptive in the way that people who have never been sure of their safety become perceptive: she reads rooms before she enters them, remembers conversations she wasn't supposed to be part of, and says less than she knows. She does not yet know what to do with any of this. She is nineteen. She has good instincts and no framework for them yet.
appearanceTags: always near the edge of the room
small notebook filled with other people's words
looks older than nineteen when focused

quirks.text: writes down what people say in meetings she attends, then reads it back to herself later
identifies the person in any group who is most afraid and watches them

motivation.publicGoal: To be useful enough to stay.
motivation.privateNeed: To understand why people like Petra or Oswin choose to keep doing what costs them everything.
motivation.immediatePressure: Not to make a mistake that proves she wasn't ready.

loyalties.note: On the periphery of his Warrens distribution network from age fifteen. He gave her tasks when she showed up without formally recruiting her. Four years of loose affiliation.

name: Petra Sunn
background: Spent six years in assessed bond service after a Compact Register audit found her household without a permit. She did not fight it — she did not know you could. After six years she ran. She has been running ever since, but not from fear. She runs toward: toward safehouses, toward the people who need what she built when she needed it, toward a version of this city where six years of her life cannot happen to someone else without a fight. The Compact has an active file on her. She knows the number. She found it.
appearanceTags: changes her hair and route every thirty days
watches people from outside a room before entering

quirks.text: maps every building she enters by sound and shadow before using its exits
knows the name and current situation of every person she has helped out of bond service

motivation.publicGoal: To make it harder for the Register to take people the way it took her.
motivation.privateNeed: To trust someone enough to stay in one place.
motivation.immediatePressure: A base of operations that cannot be found in the usual ways.

loyalties.note: Worked the same Warrens food distribution network. He went legitimate when the Compact closed it; she went underground when the Compact took her. 15+ years of mutual respect and a fundamental disagreement about what survival requires.

name: Halvard Senn
background: Twenty-two years in the city garrison. Not a scandal, not a disgrace — he was simply no longer useful to the people who restructured things, and accepted his severance before they found a reason to make it something worse. He bought a partial stake in a Pale brokerage. Found that civilian life has softer rules but harder politics. He is comfortable in both; he is not comfortable with people who cannot tell the difference between them. He knows how to command, how to be commanded, and the exact weight of both.
appearanceTags: right hand closes before a formal greeting
deliberate pause before addressing anyone by name

quirks.text: recites unit protocols aloud when observing a new operation
notes exits and choke points before anything else about a space

motivation.publicGoal: To run a clean operation and not have to explain himself to people who have not run one.
motivation.privateNeed: To believe the years he gave were worth what they cost.
motivation.immediatePressure: Work with a clear command structure and a clear objective.

loyalties.note: Overlapping garrison service for seven years. Not friends. Professional respect built under pressure. Halvard thinks Holst left a year too early; Holst thinks Halvard stayed a year too long. Neither is wrong.

name: Dael Morw
background: Twelve years as a Register compliance assessor for the Civic Compact. He personally processed hundreds of Register audits, bond assessments, and permit applications. He knows the form numbers, the loopholes, the assessors who can be persuaded, and the ones who cannot. He left after a senior assessor directed him to falsify a noble family's birth records. He complied. He kept copies. He then submitted a quiet resignation and walked out carrying documentation that multiple powerful people would prefer did not exist. He is not a hero. He is not a villain. He is a man who made one wrong choice and then made one correct choice, and cannot fully decide which one he is living with.
appearanceTags: ink-stained cuffs he has stopped trying to bleach
leather folio never put down in public

quirks.text: reads every document he receives twice before acknowledging it
remembers the form number of every filing he has ever processed

motivation.publicGoal: To use what he knows to help people the Register has harmed.
motivation.privateNeed: To believe that what he kept is enough to matter, when it matters.
motivation.immediatePressure: Distance from the building he worked in for twelve years and the person he was there.

loyalties.note: Knows which Register records were altered during Holst's bribery scandal. Didn't alter them but signed the review approving them. Both carry this.

name: Evar Koss
background: Ran cargo ledgers for three different noble houses over twenty years before all three collapsed, turned on him, or quietly replaced him with someone cheaper. He was not fired — he was simply not rehired. He knows the difference. He now operates as a neutral broker: moving goods, information, and occasionally people between parties who cannot be seen dealing directly. He takes no sides. He takes percentages. He is the kind of man whose name appears in four different people's private accounts and none of their official ones.

quirks.text: stores information by who told him, not by what it was
never carries anything he cannot explain if searched

motivation.publicGoal: To remain useful to enough parties that none of them can afford to remove him.
motivation.privateNeed: To believe there is still a version of this where he got out clean.

loyalties.note: Her first handler at age eleven — set her rates, taught her the courier's code. She got better than him at the work. He knows it. 15+ years.

name: Irenne Brek
background: Third-generation harbor herbalist. Her grandmother grew medicinals in the soil pockets along the harbor wall; her mother sold them from a cart; she runs a shop that is not quite a shop — no permit, no Compact license, never will be. She treats wounds, manages fevers, and provides things people cannot afford to name at a Compact clinic. She asks no questions about how injuries happened. She is built into the ward the way a support beam is — unremarkable until the moment it matters.
appearanceTags: hands that smell of comfrey and pine resin
never seems surprised by what walks through her door

quirks.text: diagnoses by listening, not looking — watches breathing and posture before anything else
keeps a running count of every person she has treated and their outcome

motivation.publicGoal: To keep the ward's people alive and as healthy as they can afford to be.
motivation.privateNeed: To believe that proximity to suffering has not made her numb to it.

loyalties.note: Has been patching Dara since she was eleven. Maternal proximity without either naming it. Dara comes here when injured before she goes anywhere else. 15+ years.

name: Tessaly Wode
background: Master glassworker who spent thirty years running the Ironworks' precision instrument trade. Knows how to read a room the way she reads a lens — cracks, distortions, what light reveals that the surface hides. Has trained a dozen engineers who no longer credit her. Does not hold it against them personally; holds it against the institution.
appearanceTags: glass-dust in the creases of her hands
spectacles worn on a cord around her neck

quirks.text: measures twice before speaking, once before acting
keeps a small lens in her pocket and holds it to the light when thinking

motivation.publicGoal: To pass on the craft before the Ironworks forgets what precision means.
motivation.privateNeed: To be seen as someone who built things, not merely someone who was used to build them.

loyalties.note: Sable cut corners on a filtration job Tessaly had spec'd. People got sick. Tessaly reported it; Sable was fined, not dismissed. The grudge runs one direction only — Sable sees an obstacle, Tessaly sees a lesson no one learned.

name: Elyn
background: Seven years old. Brought to the house by Petra Sunn. Her situation was not safe where it was. Petra does not give many details. Elyn does not talk much yet. She watches. She has her mother's hands — Petra does not say whose mother.
appearanceTags: always has something in her hands — a pebble, a piece of cord, someone else's forgotten button
takes up much less space than she has opinions

quirks.text: goes still and quiet when something frightens her, rather than fleeing
asks questions only after everyone else has stopped talking

motivation.publicGoal: To understand why things are the way they are.
motivation.privateNeed: To know if this place is somewhere she is allowed to stay.

loyalties.note: Petra moved her. Elyn does not know the word for what Petra is to her. She knows Petra came when it mattered.

name: Bren Aldoth
background: Fourteen years in a specialized Compact enforcement unit — the kind called in when public enforcement has already tried and failed. Good at it. Very good. The last operation ended badly in a way that was not his fault, not exactly, and that he cannot fully account for and will not try to. Three of his unit did not come back. He filed the report. He accepted the severance. He has not spoken to anyone from the unit since.
appearanceTags: makes eye contact slightly too long then breaks it
carries nothing that is not necessary

quirks.text: counts exits and headcounts in any new location before relaxing
asks the names of people who died in operations he was not part of

motivation.publicGoal: To be useful in a way that does not require him to think about why.
motivation.privateNeed: To find out if he is still someone worth trusting.

loyalties.note: Both came out of Compact enforcement in different units. They know each other's names and reputation. Not friends — professional acknowledgment. Holst is one of the few people from that world Bren does not actively avoid.

name: Nessa Vain
background: Spent eight years as a district resource coordinator for the Compact — the person who managed which water distribution points got funding, which wall sections got inspected, which rebuild contracts went to which crews. She learned, in those eight years, exactly how the city's physical layer and its power layer interact. She left the Compact when she understood that the Compact's interest in this knowledge was narrower than her own. She does not announce her plans. She makes moves.
appearanceTags: carries annotated district maps she does not show people
addresses problems in terms of leverage points, not solutions
remembers every piece of infrastructure she has personally assessed

quirks.text: mentally prices the replacement cost of every structure she enters
does not ask for permission for small actions — reports their outcomes instead

motivation.publicGoal: To make the house a durable presence in the city, not a temporary one.
motivation.privateNeed: To build something that will still be standing after she is not relevant to it.

loyalties.note: Both moved in Compact administrative channels. Marion filed; Nessa coordinated. Professional wariness — Marion recognizes someone who operates the way she herself does, and finds it uncomfortable.

name: Caevis Sable-Cairn
description: Head of House Sable-Cairn. The household's seal is still worth something. He knows why, and he knows how much longer it will last.
background: Third generation of a household that survived the Breach by being useful to whoever won. The piety is real. The debts are also real. The two do not cancel each other out. His seal is still worth something in civic filings and in Compact corridors. He knows how much longer that will hold, and has not decided what to do when it does not.
appearanceTags: formal blacks with a white chapel collar
rings on every finger that are not ostentatious enough to beg comment

quirks.text: speaks only in formal register — never contracts, never colloquialisms
never names his creditors aloud, only refers to 'prior obligations'

motivation.publicGoal: Outlast the debt without openly breaking with any faction.
motivation.privateNeed: Maintain the seal's value long enough to pass something to his heirs.
motivation.immediatePressure: A Compact audit of the Salt Ledger Hall will surface his household's debt within three seasons.

loyalties.note: Sorn's consolidation of Northbank power is the mechanism through which his household declines. Neither will say this to the other.

name: Tomas Rell
background: Court enforcer lieutenant running muscle for House Aurevaine's enforcement contracts. Practical, not cruel — cruelty wastes time and leaves witnesses in bad moods.

name: Catrin Hale
background: A Court warder assigned to deniable holding sites. She keeps doors, counts keys, and treats signed paper as more reliable than any witness.
